mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of instances following a 0.5pp unemployment surge, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 2% but housing starts decline, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of weeks following positive industrial production surprises, or the signal loses predictive power when VIX exceeds 25.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy expectations, increasing real discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months following CPI acceleration, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns when core PCE remains below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained macroeconomic growth that translates directly into higher corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >50% of periods following positive GDP revisions, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals supply-side weakness and impending economic slowdown, prompting defensive portfolio rebalancing and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of weeks following negative industrial production readings, or the signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns when Treasury yields decline concurrently.
